results.description: applyAxioms:41 → applyP2:120: PROVEN: extractDeclaredVars guard ensures only contracts wit
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does applyAxioms:41 establish preconditions for applyP2:120?
; Property: PROVEN: extractDeclaredVars guard ensures only contracts with SMT variable declarations
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 120
; PROVEN: extractDeclaredVars guard ensures only contracts with SMT variable declarations
;         contribute checks — zero-var contracts are silently skipped

(declare-const vars_length Int)
(declare-const checks_pushed Int)

; Precondition: vars_length is the count returned by extractDeclaredVars
(assert (>= vars_length 0))

; Transition: if (vars.length === 0) continue  — no push occurs
(assert (=> (= vars_length 0) (= checks_pushed 0)))

; Transition: if vars_length > 0, exactly one check is pushed per proven property
(assert (=> (> vars_length 0) (= checks_pushed 1)))

; Negation: a zero-var contract still produces a check
(assert (= vars_length 0))
(assert (> checks_pushed 0))

(check-sat)

; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 261
; PROVEN: blocks with assertLines.length < 2 are always excluded before self-check
; Precondition: assertLines extracted from a proven property's SMT2
; Transition: early-continue guard fires before verifyBlock is called
; Negated: verifyBlock called on a block with fewer than 2 asserts

results.description: Public input violation at checkConsistency:261: checkConsistency receives Contract[] from callers with no SM
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 261
; checkConsistency receives Contract[] from callers with no SMT2 content validation.
; Contract.proven[i].smt2 is an arbitrary string — can be malformed.
; verifyBlock on malformed SMT2 returns "error", not "unsat".
; Guard: if (selfResult.result === "unsat") continue
;   → "error" != "unsat" → block is NOT skipped → self-contradictory block included.
; Code transitions:
;   - smt2_valid = 0 (malformed/unparseable content from input contract)
;   - selfResult = "error" (Z3 rejects the SMT2)
;   - guard fires only on "unsat" → block_included = 1
(declare-const smt2_valid Int)       ; 1 = parseable by Z3, 0 = malformed
(declare-const self_result Int)      ; 0=sat, 1=unsat, 2=unknown, 3=error
(declare-const block_included Int)   ; 1 = pushed to namespacedBlocks
; Code transition: malformed SMT2 causes Z3 to return error (3)
(assert (= smt2_valid 0))
(assert (=> (= smt2_valid 0) (= self_result 3)))
; Code transition: guard only excludes on unsat (1)
(assert (=> (= self_result 1) (= block_included 0)))
(assert (=> (not (= self_result 1)) (= block_included 1)))
; Reachable violation: malformed block included in merged consistency check
(assert (= self_result 3))
(assert (= block_included 1))
(check-sat)

results.description: applyAxioms:41 → checks.map:44: PROVEN: verdict is always in {proven=0, violation=1, error=2
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does applyAxioms:41 establish preconditions for checks.map:44?
; Property: PROVEN: verdict is always in {proven=0, violation=1, error=2}
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 44
; PROVEN: verdict is always in {proven=0, violation=1, error=2}
; The nested ternary at lines 47-50 covers every (expected, result) pair.

(declare-const expected Int)  ; 0=unsat, 1=sat
(declare-const result  Int)   ; 0=unsat, 1=sat, 2=unknown, 3=error
(declare-const verdict Int)   ; 0=proven, 1=violation, 2=error

; Domain constraints
(assert (or (= expected 0) (= expected 1)))
(assert (>= result 0))
(assert (<= result 3))

; Code transition: expected=0 (unsat) branch
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (= result 0))
            (= verdict 0)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (= result 1))
            (= verdict 1)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (and (not (= result 0)) (not (= result 1))))
            (= verdict 2)))

; Code transition: expected=1 (sat) branch
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (= result 1))
            (= verdict 1)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (= result 0))
            (= verdict 0)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (and (not (= result 0)) (not (= result 1))))
            (= verdict 2)))

; Negation: verdict escapes the valid range
(assert (or (< verdict 0) (> verdict 2)))
(check-sat)

results.description: applyAxioms:41 → checks.map:44: PROVEN: A definitive Z3 result (sat or unsat) never yields v
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does applyAxioms:41 establish preconditions for checks.map:44?
; Property: PROVEN: A definitive Z3 result (sat or unsat) never yields verdict=error.
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 44
; PROVEN: A definitive Z3 result (sat or unsat) never yields verdict=error.
; verdict=error is only reachable when result=unknown or result=error (2,3).

(declare-const expected Int)  ; 0=unsat, 1=sat
(declare-const result  Int)   ; 0=unsat (0) or 1=sat ONLY
(declare-const verdict Int)

; Domain: expected is binary; result is definitive
(assert (or (= expected 0) (= expected 1)))
(assert (or (= result 0) (= result 1)))

; Code transitions: both branches of the ternary, definitive results only
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (= result 0)) (= verdict 0)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 0) (= result 1)) (= verdict 1)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (= result 1)) (= verdict 1)))
(assert (=> (and (= expected 1) (= result 0)) (= verdict 0)))

; Negation: verdict = error
(assert (= verdict 2))
(check-sat)

results.description: Public input violation at <anonymous>.catch:590: err: unknown — .message is accessed with no null/undefined g
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 590
; err: unknown — .message is accessed with no null/undefined guard
; Any Promise rejection value reaches this handler; null and undefined are valid rejection values.
; Accessing .message on null or undefined throws TypeError, aborting the catch body
; before process.exit(1) is called.
(declare-const err_is_nullish Int)        ; 1 = err is null or undefined
(declare-const property_access_safe Int)  ; 1 = err.message does not throw
(declare-const exit_reached Int)          ; 1 = process.exit(1) is executed
; Code transition: JS property access on null/undefined throws TypeError
(assert (=> (= err_is_nullish 1) (= property_access_safe 0)))
; Code transition: TypeError in console.error(...) call aborts catch body before process.exit
(assert (=> (= property_access_safe 0) (= exit_reached 0)))
; Calling context: err is null — a valid Promise.reject(null) value; no guard in the handler
(assert (= err_is_nullish 1))
; Violation: process.exit(1) is never called — process exits via unhandled secondary TypeError
(assert (= exit_reached 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: main:22 → fileIssues:518: "Filing N issues" — N is a sat-only count. collectViolationI

; P1 MECHANICAL: Does main:22 establish preconditions for fileIssues:518?
; Property: "Filing N issues" — N is a sat-only count. collectViolationIssues filters
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 518

; verifications to z3Result === "sat"; unsat (proven) results are excluded.

(declare-const sat_count Int)
(declare-const unsat_count Int)
(declare-const issues_length Int)

(assert (>= sat_count 0))
(assert (>= unsat_count 0))
(assert (= total_verifications (+ sat_count unsat_count)))

; Code transition: collectViolationIssues filters to sat only
(assert (= issues_length sat_count))

; Negate: could issues_length equal total (include unsat)?
(assert (> unsat_count 0))
(assert (= issues_length total_verifications))

results.description: fileIssues:518 → fileIssues:520: Conservation law: every issue in issues[] contributes exactl
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does fileIssues:518 establish preconditions for fileIssues:520?
; Property: Conservation law: every issue in issues[] contributes exactly once to
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 520
; Conservation law: every issue in issues[] contributes exactly once to
; filed, skipped, or errors. Loop in fileViolationIssues has five branches:
;   A: seen.has(title) → skipped++, continue
;   B: dryRun         → filed++, continue
;   C: issueExists()  → skipped++, continue
;   D: fileIssue ok   → filed++
;   E: fileIssue err  → errors++
; Each branch increments exactly one counter. No branch is skipped entirely.

(declare-const issues_length Int)
(declare-const filed Int)
(declare-const skipped Int)
(declare-const errors Int)

; Code transition: issues_length items enter the loop
(assert (>= issues_length 0))
(assert (>= filed 0))
(assert (>= skipped 0))
(assert (>= errors 0))

; Code transition: every loop iteration takes exactly one branch (above)
(assert (= (+ filed skipped errors) issues_length))

; Negate the conservation law

; Conservation law: every issue in issues[] contributes exactly once to
; filed, skipped, or errors. Loop in fileViolationIssues has five branches:
;   A: seen.has(title) → skipped++, continue
;   B: dryRun         → filed++, continue
;   C: issueExists()  → skipped++, continue

;   E: fileIssue err  → errors++
; Each branch increments exactly one counter. No branch is skipped entirely.

(declare-const filed Int)
(declare-const skipped Int)
(declare-const errors Int)

; Code transition: issues_length items enter the loop
(assert (>= issues_length 0))
(assert (>= filed 0))
(assert (>= skipped 0))
(assert (>= errors 0))

; Code transition: every loop iteration takes exactly one branch (above)
(assert (= (+ filed skipped errors) issues_length))

; Negate the conservation law
(assert (not (= (+ filed skipped errors) issues_length)))
(check-sat)

results.description: main:22 → main:40: command is provably defined (non-null) at line 40 — the non-
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does main:22 establish preconditions for main:40?
; Property: command is provably defined (non-null) at line 40 — the non-null assertion args[0]! is safe.
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 40
; command is provably defined (non-null) at line 40 — the non-null assertion args[0]! is safe.
; PROVE: command_defined = 1 is invariant at line 40.
(declare-const args_length Int)
(declare-const command_defined Int)   ; 1 = args[0] is a defined string value
; Code transition: guard at line 16 calls process.exit(0) when args.length === 0
; Reaching line 40 means that guard was false → args_length > 0
(assert (> args_length 0))
; Code transition: command = args[0]! — TypeScript non-null assertion; args_length > 0 makes it safe
(assert (=> (> args_length 0) (= command_defined 1)))
; Negation: command is undefined at line 40
(assert (= command_defined 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: Public input violation at main:40: command = args[0] is fully caller-supplied. The "-v" verbose
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 40
; command = args[0] is fully caller-supplied. The "-v" verbose flag is documented in --help
; but if passed as args[0], it is reported as "Unknown command: -v" instead of being recognized.
(declare-const args_0_is_verbose_flag Int)  ; 1 = args[0] is "-v" (documented verbose flag)
(declare-const is_help_flag Int)             ; 1 = args[0] is "--help" or "-h"
(declare-const is_version_flag_present Int)  ; 1 = args.includes("--version") anywhere
(declare-const reaches_default Int)          ; 1 = switch default branch executes
; Code transition: "-v" is not "--help" or "-h" → line 16 guard does NOT exit
(assert (=> (= args_0_is_verbose_flag 1) (= is_help_flag 0)))
; Code transition: "-v" is not "--version" → line 20 guard does NOT exit
(assert (=> (= args_0_is_verbose_flag 1) (= is_version_flag_present 0)))
; Code transition: switch has no case for "-v" → default executes
(assert (=> (= args_0_is_verbose_flag 1) (= reaches_default 1)))
; args[0] = "-v" (user ran "neurallog -v analyze src/foo.ts")
(assert (= args_0_is_verbose_flag 1))
; Violation: a documented option is reported as an unknown command
(assert (= args_0_is_verbose_flag 1))
(assert (= reaches_default 1))
(check-sat)

results.description: buildSignalRegistry:529 → printHelp:537: VERSION = "0.3.0" is a compile-time constant (5 chars).
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does buildSignalRegistry:529 establish preconditions for printHelp:537?

; LINE: 537
; VERSION = "0.3.0" is a compile-time constant (5 chars).
; The interpolated header "neurallog v0.3.0 — a logger that fixes your code"
; must therefore be provably non-empty.
(declare-const version_len Int)
(declare-const prefix_len Int)
(declare-const suffix_len Int)
(declare-const header_len Int)
; Code transition: VERSION literal "0.3.0" has exactly 5 characters

(assert (= prefix_len 11))
; Code transition: suffix " — a logger that fixes your code" is positive in length
(assert (> suffix_len 0))
; Code transition: header_len = prefix + VERSION + suffix
(assert (= header_len (+ prefix_len version_len suffix_len)))
; Negation of property: header is empty
(assert (<= header_len 0))
(check-sat)

; P1 MECHANICAL: Does runVerify:251 establish preconditions for printHelp:537?
; Property: Ordering constraint: printHelp contains zero if-statements and zero
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 537
; Ordering constraint: printHelp contains zero if-statements and zero
; return-statements. No code path can bypass any of the 24 log calls.
(declare-const if_stmt_count Int)
(declare-const return_stmt_count Int)
(declare-const skippable_calls Int)
; Code transition: AST of printHelp body has 0 if-statements
(assert (= if_stmt_count 0))
; Code transition: AST of printHelp body has 0 return-statements
(assert (= return_stmt_count 0))
; Code transition: calls that CAN be skipped = branches + early returns
(assert (= skippable_calls (+ if_stmt_count return_stmt_count)))
; Negation: at least one log call can be skipped
(assert (> skippable_calls 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: Public input violation at printSummary:496: VIOLATION: result.graph is typed 'any' — no null guard befor
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 496
; VIOLATION: result.graph is typed 'any' — no null guard before .files.length access.
; The calling code (runAnalyze / runDerive) passes pipeline.runFull() result verbatim;
; printSummary never validates that result.graph or result.graph.files exists.
(declare-const graph_null Int)           ; 1 = result.graph is null or undefined
(declare-const files_length_throws Int)  ; 1 = accessing .files.length raises TypeError
; Code transition: result.graph.files.length is read directly at line 496
;                  no guard: no (if result.graph && result.graph.files) check exists
(assert (=> (= graph_null 1) (= files_length_throws 1)))
; Caller supplies any-typed value; graph being null is unconstrained by printSummary
(assert (= graph_null 1))
(assert (= files_length_throws 1))
(check-sat)

results.description: Public input violation at printSummary:496: VIOLATION: result.derivation is typed 'any' — no null guard
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 496
; VIOLATION: result.derivation is typed 'any' — no null guard before .contracts.length.
; Same pattern as graph: runDerive could return a result where derivation is missing.
(declare-const derivation_null Int)          ; 1 = result.derivation is null/undefined
(declare-const contracts_access_throws Int)  ; 1 = .contracts.length throws TypeError
; Code transition: result.derivation.contracts.length accessed directly (line 496)
;                  result.derivation.contracts.reduce(...) accessed directly (line 497)
;                  no null guard anywhere in printSummary
(assert (=> (= derivation_null 1) (= contracts_access_throws 1)))
(assert (= derivation_null 1))
(assert (= contracts_access_throws 1))
(check-sat)

results.description: Boundary violation at printSummary:497: VIOLATION: If contracts is an empty array [], both reduces r
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 497
; VIOLATION: If contracts is an empty array [], both reduces return 0 immediately.
; The display "0 proven | 0 violations" is printed as if derivation completed normally.
; This masks a pipeline failure where no contracts were derived (silent empty result).
(declare-const num_contracts Int)
(declare-const phase3_proven Int)
(declare-const phase3_violations Int)
; Code transition: reduce([]) with initial value 0 returns 0 — JavaScript semantics
(assert (= num_contracts 0))
(assert (= phase3_proven 0))
(assert (= phase3_violations 0))
; Degenerate state: summary is printed as complete; reader cannot distinguish
; "derivation found no signals" from "derivation failed and returned empty"
(assert (and (= phase3_proven 0) (= phase3_violations 0)))
(check-sat)

results.description: Public input violation at runAnalyze:131: VIOLATION: "File: X" — X can be the current working director
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 131
; VIOLATION: "File: X" — X can be the current working directory when args has no positional arg
(declare-const positional_arg_count Int)    ; number of args not starting with "-"
(declare-const filePath_is_directory Int)   ; 1 = filePath is a directory, not a TS file
; Code transition: args.find(a => !a.startsWith("-")) returns undefined when count = 0
(assert (= positional_arg_count 0))
; Code transition: undefined || "." fires → resolve(".") = cwd = a directory
(assert (=> (= positional_arg_count 0) (= filePath_is_directory 1)))
; Violation: log says "File:" but value is a directory
(assert (= filePath_is_directory 1))
(check-sat)

results.description: Public input violation at runAnalyze:132: PROVEN: model is always a non-empty string — || "sonnet" fal
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 132
; PROVEN: model is always a non-empty string — || "sonnet" fallback covers all falsy returns
(declare-const getflag_return_length Int)  ; 0 = undefined/empty, >0 = non-empty value
(declare-const model_length Int)           ; length of model string after || operator
; Code transition: getFlag returns undefined when --model absent or is last element
; Code transition: empty string is also falsy → || fires
(assert (=> (= getflag_return_length 0) (= model_length 6)))  ; "sonnet" has length 6
; Code transition: non-empty getFlag return → model = that return (same non-zero length)
(assert (=> (> getflag_return_length 0) (= model_length getflag_return_length)))
; Negation: model is empty (length 0)
(assert (= model_length 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: Public input violation at runAnalyze:132: VIOLATION: model can be any caller-supplied string — no allo
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 132
; VIOLATION: model can be any caller-supplied string — no allowlist check against valid model names
(declare-const flag_present Int)               ; 1 = --model flag appears in args
(declare-const flag_next_value_is_valid Int)   ; 1 = value is "sonnet"/"haiku"/"opus"/known model
(declare-const model_is_valid Int)             ; 1 = model is a recognized provider model name
; Code transition: getFlag returns args[idx + 1] without any validation of the string content
(assert (= flag_present 1))
(assert (= flag_next_value_is_valid 0))     ; caller passed an unrecognized model name
; Code transition: no allowlist; model = whatever the caller supplied
(assert (= model_is_valid flag_next_value_is_valid))
; Violation: model logged as fact, but its value is semantically invalid
(assert (= model_is_valid 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: main:22 → runAnalyze:133: PROVEN: getGeneratorNames().join(", ") always produces a str
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does main:22 establish preconditions for runAnalyze:133?
; Property: PROVEN: getGeneratorNames().join(", ") always produces a string — join() is total over arrays
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 133
; PROVEN: getGeneratorNames().join(", ") always produces a string — join() is total over arrays
(declare-const names_array_is_defined Int)  ; 1 = getGeneratorNames() returns a value (not throws)
(declare-const join_result_is_string Int)   ; 1 = join() returned a string value
; Code transition: TypeScript return type of getGeneratorNames() is string[]
; a typed method cannot return undefined without a type error under strict mode
(assert (= names_array_is_defined 1))
; Code transition: Array.prototype.join is defined for all arrays including []
; join([]) = "", join(["a","b"]) = "a, b" — never throws, always returns string
(assert (=> (= names_array_is_defined 1) (= join_result_is_string 1)))
; Negation: join throws or returns non-string
(assert (= join_result_is_string 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: Boundary violation at runAnalyze:133: VIOLATION: if createDefault() registers 0 generators, join("
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 133
; VIOLATION: if createDefault() registers 0 generators, join("") → "Signals: " (degenerate empty)
(declare-const default_generator_count Int)    ; generators registered by createDefault()
(declare-const llm_signals_flag Int)           ; 1 = --llm-signals in args
(declare-const total_generator_count Int)      ; total after buildSignalRegistry completes
(declare-const signals_output_length Int)      ; byte length of the join result
; Code transition: buildSignalRegistry calls SignalRegistry.createDefault()
; count is non-negative (registry can hold 0 generators)
(assert (>= default_generator_count 0))
; Code transition: --llm-signals absent → no additional generator registered
(assert (= llm_signals_flag 0))
(assert (= total_generator_count default_generator_count))
; Code transition: join([]) = "" when generator_count = 0
(assert (= default_generator_count 0))
(assert (=> (= total_generator_count 0) (= signals_output_length 0)))
; Degenerate: log prints "Signals: " — empty string where signal names are expected
(assert (= signals_output_length 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: Public input violation at runDiff:267: Prove: ref is non-empty — code: const ref = args.find(...) |
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 267
; Prove: ref is non-empty — code: const ref = args.find(...) || "HEAD~1"
; find() returns undefined (no match) or a string; || "HEAD~1" covers all falsy cases

(declare-const find_returned Int)    ; 0=undefined, 1=empty_string, 2=nonempty_string
(declare-const ref_nonempty Int)     ; 1 = ref has at least one character

; Transition: ref = find_result || "HEAD~1"
; find returns undefined → ref = "HEAD~1" (non-empty)
(assert (=> (= find_returned 0) (= ref_nonempty 1)))
; find returns "" (falsy) → ref = "HEAD~1" (non-empty)
(assert (=> (= find_returned 1) (= ref_nonempty 1)))
; find returns non-empty string (truthy) → ref = that string (non-empty)
(assert (=> (= find_returned 2) (= ref_nonempty 1)))

; Attempt to falsify: ref is empty at line 267
(assert (= ref_nonempty 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: Public input violation at runDiff:267: args is user-supplied; ref = first non-flag arg with no form
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 267
; args is user-supplied; ref = first non-flag arg with no format check
; diffAgainst(ref) requires a valid git ref as precondition
; runDiff does not establish that precondition

(declare-const user_provided_positional Int)  ; 1 = user gave a positional arg
(declare-const user_ref_valid Int)            ; 1 = user's string is a valid git ref
(declare-const ref_valid Int)                 ; 1 = ref reaching diffAgainst is valid

; Transition: no positional arg → default "HEAD~1" (valid)
(assert (=> (= user_provided_positional 0) (= ref_valid 1)))
; Transition: positional arg present → ref = that arg, validity unconstrained by code
(assert (=> (= user_provided_positional 1) (= ref_valid user_ref_valid)))

; Violation: user passes an unrecognised string (e.g. "not@@a//ref")
(assert (= user_provided_positional 1))
(assert (= user_ref_valid 0))
(assert (= ref_valid 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: main:22 → runDiff:268: Prove: both variables are bound before the blank-line log ex
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does main:22 establish preconditions for runDiff:268?
; Property: Prove: both variables are bound before the blank-line log executes
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 268
; Prove: both variables are bound before the blank-line log executes
; resolveProjectRoot always returns (falls back to cwd, never throws for missing dir)
; ref always binds via || "HEAD~1" fallback

(declare-const project_root_bound Int)  ; 1 = resolveProjectRoot returned a string
(declare-const ref_bound Int)           ; 1 = ref is assigned a string

; Transition: resolveProjectRoot walks up to cwd as final fallback — always returns
(assert (= project_root_bound 1))
; Transition: args.find(...) || "HEAD~1" — always assigns a value
(assert (= ref_bound 1))

; Attempt to falsify: either variable is unbound at the blank log
(assert (or (= project_root_bound 0) (= ref_bound 0)))
(check-sat)

; Property: ternary: regressions === 1 ? "" : "s"
; PRINCIPLE: P5

; ternary: regressions === 1 ? "" : "s"
; Prove: suffix is "" iff regressions == 1; suffix is "s" iff regressions > 1
; (regressions < 1 is ruled out by the path-condition proof above)

(assert (=> (> regressions 1) (= suffix_is_plural 1)))

; Attempt to falsify: singular suffix used while regressions > 1
(assert (= suffix_is_plural 0))
(assert (> regressions 1))
(check-sat)

; Property: Proven: at line 390, violations_length > 0 and v.smt2 was processed
; PRINCIPLE: P3

; (line 390 is inside the violations loop — same guard as line 376)

; P1 MECHANICAL: Does main:22 establish preconditions for runHook:470?
; Property: PROVEN: line 470 (default/install branch) executes only when --uninstall, --remove,
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 470
; PROVEN: line 470 (default/install branch) executes only when --uninstall, --remove,
; and --status are all absent. Negate → expect unsat.

(declare-const flag_uninstall  Int)
(declare-const flag_remove     Int)
(declare-const flag_status     Int)
(declare-const line470_reached Int)

; Code transition: else (default) arm fires only when both prior guards fail

           (ite (and (= flag_uninstall 0)
                     (= flag_remove   0)
                     (= flag_status   0))
                1 0)))

; Negation: line 470 reached but at least one flag is present
(assert (= line470_reached 1))
(assert (or (= flag_uninstall 1) (= flag_remove 1) (= flag_status 1)))

(check-sat)

; P1 MECHANICAL: Does main:22 establish preconditions for runInit:97?
; Property: PROVEN: totalSignals >= fileCount at the time of the log.
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 97
; PROVEN: totalSignals >= fileCount at the time of the log.
; fileCount increments only when signals.length > 0, adding at least 1 to totalSignals.
(declare-const total_signals Int)
(declare-const file_count Int)
(declare-const n Int)  ; number of counted files
(declare-const s Int)  ; minimum signals per counted file
; Code transition: s >= 1 (guard: `if (signals.length > 0)`)
(assert (>= s 1))
; Code transition: file_count = n; total_signals >= n * s (each counted file contributes >= 1)
(assert (>= n 0))
(assert (= file_count n))
(assert (>= total_signals (* n s)))
; Negated property: totalSignals < fileCount
(assert (< total_signals file_count))
(check-sat)

results.description: Boundary violation at runInit:98: VIOLATION: if createDefault() registers no generators, getGe

results.description: Public input violation at runOverride:482: Violation: empty string from getFlag is falsy — flag present

; Violation: empty string from getFlag is falsy — flag present, error fires anyway
(declare-const flag_present Int)      ; 1 = "--reason" exists in args
(declare-const next_arg_length Int)   ; character length of args[idx+1]
(declare-const reason_truthy Int)     ; 1 = passes !reason guard
; Code transition: getFlag returns args[idx+1] when flag found and idx+1 < length
(assert (= flag_present 1))
; Code transition: caller passed --reason "" → next arg is empty string
(assert (= next_arg_length 0))
; Code transition: empty string is falsy in JavaScript (!("") === true)
(assert (=> (= next_arg_length 0) (= reason_truthy 0)))
; Violation: flag was explicitly provided, yet the usage error fires
(assert (= reason_truthy 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: main:22 → runReport:445: PROVEN: byFile.size > 0 is guaranteed when contracts.length
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does main:22 establish preconditions for runReport:445?
; Property: PROVEN: byFile.size > 0 is guaranteed when contracts.length > 0
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 445
; PROVEN: byFile.size > 0 is guaranteed when contracts.length > 0
; The if (byFile.size > 0) guard at line 444 is provably redundant
(declare-const contracts_length Int)
(declare-const byfile_size Int)
; Code transition: contracts_length > 0 (early-exit guard passed)
(assert (> contracts_length 0))
; Code transition: the accumulation loop calls byFile.set(c.file, entry) for every contract
; At least one contract exists, so at least one set() call was made
(assert (>= byfile_size 1))
; Code transition: byfile_size is bounded above by contracts_length (one entry per unique file)
(assert (<= byfile_size contracts_length))
; Negated property: byfile_size = 0 (the guard at line 444 would be false — never happens)
(assert (= byfile_size 0))
(check-sat)

; P1 MECHANICAL: Does runReport:442 establish preconditions for runReport:445?
; Property: PROVEN: byFile.size > 0 is guaranteed when contracts.length > 0
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 445
; PROVEN: byFile.size > 0 is guaranteed when contracts.length > 0
; The if (byFile.size > 0) guard at line 444 is provably redundant
(declare-const contracts_length Int)
(declare-const byfile_size Int)
; Code transition: contracts_length > 0 (early-exit guard passed)
(assert (> contracts_length 0))
; Code transition: the accumulation loop calls byFile.set(c.file, entry) for every contract
; At least one contract exists, so at least one set() call was made
(assert (>= byfile_size 1))
; Code transition: byfile_size is bounded above by contracts_length (one entry per unique file)
(assert (<= byfile_size contracts_length))
; Negated property: byfile_size = 0 (the guard at line 444 would be false — never happens)
(assert (= byfile_size 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: main:22 → runReport:450: PROVEN: fileCoverage in [0, 100] for every file entry
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does main:22 establish preconditions for runReport:450?
; Property: PROVEN: fileCoverage in [0, 100] for every file entry
; PRINCIPLE: P7
; LINE: 450
; PROVEN: fileCoverage in [0, 100] for every file entry
(declare-const signals Int)
(declare-const unverified Int)
(declare-const numerator Int)
; Code transition: signals >= 1 — every contract adds 1 to entry.signals
; entries only exist because at least one contract mapped to this file
(assert (>= signals 1))
; Code transition: 0 <= unverified <= signals (boolean increment, once per contract)
(assert (>= unverified 0))
(assert (<= unverified signals))
; Code transition: numerator = signals - unverified
(assert (= numerator (- signals unverified)))
; Negated property: numerator < 0 (would produce negative fileCoverage)
(assert (< numerator 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: Public input violation at runVerify:243: PROVEN: projectRoot logged as "Project: ${projectRoot}" is a

; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 243
; PROVEN: projectRoot logged as "Project: ${projectRoot}" is always a non-null string.
; resolveProjectRoot returns resolve(explicit) or findProjectRoot(cwd).
; findProjectRoot traverses directories and returns startDir as an unconditional fallback.
(declare-const has_explicit_arg Int)   ; 1 = positional arg present in args
(declare-const projectRoot_null Int)   ; 1 = projectRoot is null or undefined
; Code transition: explicit path → projectRoot = resolve(explicit) — non-null string
(assert (=> (= has_explicit_arg 1) (= projectRoot_null 0)))
; Code transition: no explicit path → projectRoot = findProjectRoot(cwd).
; findProjectRoot always returns startDir as fallback — never null.
(assert (=> (= has_explicit_arg 0) (= projectRoot_null 0)))
; Negation: can projectRoot be null at line 243?
(assert (= projectRoot_null 1))
(check-sat)

; Property: PROVEN: report.violations >= 1 at line 252.
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 252
; PROVEN: report.violations >= 1 at line 252.
; The guard "if (report.violations > 0)" establishes this before the log fires.
; The ternary (violations === 1 ? "" : "s") is therefore applied to a positive integer —
; the singular/plural distinction is semantically valid.
(declare-const violations Int)
(declare-const line252_reached Int)
; Code transition: line 252 is inside if (report.violations > 0)
(assert (=> (= line252_reached 1) (> violations 0)))
; We are at line 252:
(assert (= line252_reached 1))
; Negation: can violations = 0, producing "0 violations found" with plural suppressed?
(assert (= violations 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: Public input violation at loadFromDisk:115: VIOLATION: JSON.parse output is used without schema validati
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 115
; VIOLATION: JSON.parse output is used without schema validation.
; If data.proven is null/undefined, contract.proven.map() throws at runtime.
; The throw is caught by the outer catch {}, so this.contracts.set() never executes.
; The contract is silently dropped; contracts.size undercounts.

(declare-const has_valid_key Int)     ; 1 = data.key is truthy
(declare-const proven_is_array Int)   ; 1 = data.proven is a valid Array
(declare-const set_executes Int)      ; 1 = this.contracts.set() is called
(declare-const contracts_size Int)

; Code transition: data.key is truthy → enter if(data.key) branch
(assert (= has_valid_key 1))

; Code transition: JSON.parse makes no schema guarantees — proven can be null
; (TypeScript's "as const" cast does not validate at runtime)
(assert (= proven_is_array 0))

; Code transition: .map() called on null throws TypeError
;   → outer catch {} fires → set() never called for this file
(assert (=> (= proven_is_array 0) (= set_executes 0)))
(assert (= set_executes 0))

; Code transition: no set() call → this contract absent from map
(assert (= contracts_size 0))

; Violation: file had data.key set (should have been loaded) but is silently dropped
(assert (= has_valid_key 1))
(assert (= contracts_size 0))

results.description: parseFile:40 → extractFunctionName:140: Proven: when all structural guards hold for the member_expre
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does parseFile:40 establish preconditions for extractFunctionName:140?
; Property: Proven: when all structural guards hold for the member_expression callback branch,
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 140
; Proven: when all structural guards hold for the member_expression callback branch,
; the function returns a qualified (non-anonymous) name.

(declare-const parent_is_arguments       Int)  ; 1 = node.parent.type === "arguments"
(declare-const grandparent_is_call_expr  Int)  ; 1 = node.parent.parent.type === "call_expression"
(declare-const callfn_is_member_expr     Int)  ; 1 = callFn.type === "member_expression"
(declare-const method_nonnull            Int)  ; 1 = childForFieldName("property") != null
(declare-const object_not_call_expr      Int)  ; 1 = object.type !== "call_expression"
(declare-const object_text_nonnull       Int)  ; 1 = object.text is defined
(declare-const result_is_anonymous       Int)  ; 1 = result === "<anonymous>"

; Code transition: all structural guards pass (lines 141–143)
(assert (= parent_is_arguments      1))
(assert (= grandparent_is_call_expr 1))
(assert (= callfn_is_member_expr    1))
(assert (= method_nonnull           1))
(assert (= object_not_call_expr     1))

; Code transition: tree-sitter guarantees .text is defined for any non-null SyntaxNode
(assert (= object_text_nonnull 1))

; Code transition: line 150 executes return `${objName}.${method.text}`
; — a non-empty concatenation that can never equal "<anonymous>"
(assert (=> (and (= method_nonnull 1) (= object_text_nonnull 1))
            (= result_is_anonymous 0)))

; Negation of property: result IS anonymous — prove this is impossible
(assert (= result_is_anonymous 1))
(check-sat)

results.description: Public input violation at extractFunctionName:140: Violation: extractFunctionName(object) is called where objec
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 140
; Violation: extractFunctionName(object) is called where object.type === "call_expression".
; The function's implicit precondition is that the input node is a function-defining node
; (function_declaration, arrow_function, etc.). No guard validates this before the call.
; When called with a call_expression node:
;   - childForFieldName("name") returns null (call_expressions have no "name" field)
;   - object.parent is a member_expression (not "arguments"), so the callback branch is skipped
;   - the walk-up loop starts from inside the outer member_expression's ancestry,
;     potentially returning a name that belongs to the outer call's scope, not the inner expression

(declare-const object_node_is_call_expr  Int)  ; 1 = object.type === "call_expression"
(declare-const name_field_null           Int)  ; 1 = childForFieldName("name") returns null
(declare-const object_parent_is_member   Int)  ; 1 = object.parent.type === "member_expression"
(declare-const callback_branch_entered   Int)  ; 1 = line-141 "arguments" condition fires
(declare-const walksup_executes          Int)  ; 1 = walk-up loop at line 160 runs
(declare-const walksup_returns_outer     Int)  ; 1 = walk-up finds ancestor from outer scope

; Code transition: caller passes a call_expression node (e.g., object = getObj())
(assert (= object_node_is_call_expr 1))

; Code transition: tree-sitter grammar — call_expression has fields "function" and "arguments",
; not "name"; childForFieldName("name") returns null
(assert (= name_field_null object_node_is_call_expr))

; Code transition: object is a child of a member_expression (position inside outerFn.method call),
; not a direct child of an "arguments" node at the top level
(assert (= object_parent_is_member   1))
(assert (= callback_branch_entered   (- 1 object_parent_is_member)))

; Code transition: with name_field_null=1 and callback_branch_entered=0,
; execution reaches the walk-up loop at line 160
(assert (= walksup_executes (* name_field_null (- 1 callback_branch_entered))))

; Code transition: walk-up from member_expression traverses to ancestors of the outer call;
; may find a variable_declarator or assignment_expression belonging to the outer scope
(assert (= walksup_returns_outer walksup_executes))

; Violation: precondition not established — extractFunctionName returns a name
; from the outer call's AST context, not one that identifies the inner call_expression
(assert (= walksup_executes      1))
(assert (= walksup_returns_outer 1))
(check-sat)

results.description: extractFunctionName:140 → extractFunctionName:154: Proven: when callFn is a non-null identifier, the return val
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does extractFunctionName:140 establish preconditions for extractFunctionName:154?
; Property: Proven: when callFn is a non-null identifier, the return value is well-formed.
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 154
; Proven: when callFn is a non-null identifier, the return value is well-formed.
; The optional-chaining guard (callFn?.type === "identifier") ensures callFn is non-null
; within the branch body, and tree-sitter guarantees .text for any non-null node.

(declare-const callfn_nonnull             Int)  ; 1 = callFn != null
(declare-const callfn_type_is_identifier  Int)  ; 1 = callFn.type === "identifier"
(declare-const callfn_text_defined        Int)  ; 1 = callFn.text is defined
(declare-const result_has_callback_suffix Int)  ; 1 = result === "${callFn.text}.callback"

; Code transition: optional-chaining short-circuits on null, so within the branch:
(assert (= callfn_nonnull            1))
(assert (= callfn_type_is_identifier 1))

; Code transition: tree-sitter invariant — .text always defined for non-null SyntaxNode
(assert (= callfn_text_defined callfn_nonnull))

; Code transition: line 155 executes return `${callFn.text}.callback`
(assert (=> (and (= callfn_nonnull 1) (= callfn_text_defined 1))
            (= result_has_callback_suffix 1)))

; Negation: result does NOT have the ".callback" suffix
(assert (= result_has_callback_suffix 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: Boundary violation at extractFunctionName:154: Violation: degenerate member_expression input (property fiel
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 154
; Violation: degenerate member_expression input (property field null, possible from
; tree-sitter error-recovery parses) causes BOTH the member_expression branch and the
; identifier branch to be skipped. The callback-argument block exits without returning,
; silently falling through to the walk-up loop — abandoning the programmer's intent to
; handle all callback naming patterns within lines 141–158.

(declare-const callfn_type_is_member   Int)  ; 1 = callFn.type === "member_expression"
(declare-const property_field_null     Int)  ; 1 = childForFieldName("property") === null
(declare-const member_branch_returns   Int)  ; 1 = member_expression branch fires a return
(declare-const identifier_branch_fires Int)  ; 1 = identifier branch condition holds
(declare-const callback_block_returns  Int)  ; 1 = lines 141–158 return a value

; Code transition: callFn is a member_expression (structurally correct but degenerate)
(assert (= callfn_type_is_member 1))

; Code transition: property field is null (error-recovery tree-sitter node, or a computed property
; that tree-sitter represents without a "property" named field)
(assert (= property_field_null 1))

; Code transition: line 146 — `if (method)` guard requires method != null
; member_branch_returns is 1 only when property_field_null = 0

results.description: Boundary violation at visit:295: REACHABLE: Single-line function → body_start_row = log_line
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 295
; REACHABLE: Single-line function → body_start_row = log_line → visit(body) returns immediately.
; The entire local-type inference pass produces an empty map despite declarations existing.

(declare-const body_start_row Int)
(declare-const log_line Int)
(declare-const num_decls_in_body Int)   ; declarations present in the function body
(declare-const types_entry_count Int)   ; entries written into the types map

; Code transition: logLine = logNode.startPosition.row (single-line function body)
(assert (= log_line 0))

; Code transition: body node starts on same row (opening brace on same line as log)
(assert (= body_start_row log_line))

; Code transition: body contains at least one declaration that could be typed
(assert (> num_decls_in_body 0))

; Code transition: visit(body) fires guard immediately — no children visited, no entries written
(assert (=> (>= body_start_row log_line) (= types_entry_count 0)))

; Violation: types is empty even though declarations exist
(assert (= types_entry_count 0))

(check-sat)

results.description: isVacuous:59 → isTrivialIdentity:94: PROVEN: early return false is sound — fewer than 2 asserts c
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does isVacuous:59 establish preconditions for isTrivialIdentity:94?
; Property: PROVEN: early return false is sound — fewer than 2 asserts cannot form a trivial identity
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 94
; PROVEN: early return false is sound — fewer than 2 asserts cannot form a trivial identity
; A trivial identity requires (assert P) + (assert (not P)) → at least 2 asserts

(declare-const assert_count Int)
(declare-const result Int)             ; 1 = true, 0 = false
(declare-const is_genuine_trivial Int) ; 1 = formula truly has P and ¬P

; Code transition: early return branch
(assert (< assert_count 2))
(assert (= result 0))

; Domain invariant: a genuine P ∧ ¬P pattern needs at least one equality assert
; and one negation assert — that is, at least 2 assert statements
(assert (=> (= is_genuine_trivial 1) (>= assert_count 2)))

; Negate the property: can the formula be a genuine trivial identity
; when assert_count < 2 and result = 0?
(assert (= is_genuine_trivial 1))
(check-sat)

results.description: isVacuous:59 → isTrivialIdentity:94: PROVEN: when atomic equality and matching negation are both
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does isVacuous:59 establish preconditions for isTrivialIdentity:94?
; Property: PROVEN: when atomic equality and matching negation are both regex-captured,
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 94
; PROVEN: when atomic equality and matching negation are both regex-captured,
; the function correctly returns true (the commuted-pair check is exhaustive
; for the two-token case)

(declare-const eq_pair_stored Int)   ; 1 = "A B" entered equalities list
(declare-const neg_pair_found Int)   ; 1 = "A B" or "B A" in negations list
(declare-const result Int)           ; 1 = true, 0 = false

; Code transition: equality regex matched, pair pushed to equalities
(assert (= eq_pair_stored 1))

; Code transition: loop checks negations.includes(eq) OR negations.includes(b+" "+a)
; if either holds, returns true immediately
(assert (= neg_pair_found 1))

; Code transition: match found in loop body → result = true
(assert (=> (and (= eq_pair_stored 1) (= neg_pair_found 1)) (= result 1)))

; Negate: can result = false despite both sides matching?
(assert (= result 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: Public input violation at verifyBlock:32: VIOLATION: verifyBlock passes smt2 directly to execSync with
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 32
; VIOLATION: verifyBlock passes smt2 directly to execSync with no input validation.
; An empty (assertionless) string makes z3 output "sat" (empty theory is trivially satisfiable).
; verifyBlock then returns { result: "sat" } having verified nothing.
(declare-const assert_count Int)          ; (assert ...) forms present in smt2
(declare-const z3_outputs_sat Int)        ; 1 = z3 stdout line is "sat"
(declare-const classify_returns_sat Int)  ; 1 = classify() resolves to sat

; Code transition: no guard on assert_count in verifyBlock — any value accepted
; Code transition: z3 on assertionless input → trivially satisfiable → outputs "sat"
(assert (= assert_count 0))
(assert (= z3_outputs_sat 1))

; Code transition: classify backward scan matches "sat" line → returns sat
(assert (=> (= z3_outputs_sat 1) (= classify_returns_sat 1)))

; Violation: function reports sat while zero assertions were evaluated
(assert (= classify_returns_sat 1))
(assert (= assert_count 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: State mutation between applyP2:102 and applyP2:113: PROVEN: Map-with-push partitions input — sum of group sizes
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 102
; PROVEN: Map-with-push partitions input — sum of group sizes equals total input size

(declare-const total_contracts Int)
(declare-const sum_group_sizes Int)
(declare-const num_groups Int)

; Precondition: non-negative, finite input
(assert (>= total_contracts 0))
(assert (>= num_groups 0))
(assert (<= num_groups total_contracts))

; Transition: each iteration picks exactly one key and pushes exactly one contract
; → sum of all group sizes equals total_contracts
(assert (= sum_group_sizes total_contracts))

; Negation of property
(assert (not (= sum_group_sizes total_contracts)))

(check-sat)

results.description: State mutation between applyP2:120 and applyP2:121: PROVEN: extractDeclaredVars guard ensures only contracts wit
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 120
; PROVEN: extractDeclaredVars guard ensures only contracts with SMT variable declarations
;         contribute checks — zero-var contracts are silently skipped

(declare-const vars_length Int)
(declare-const checks_pushed Int)

; Precondition: vars_length is the count returned by extractDeclaredVars
(assert (>= vars_length 0))

; Transition: if (vars.length === 0) continue  — no push occurs
(assert (=> (= vars_length 0) (= checks_pushed 0)))

; Transition: if vars_length > 0, exactly one check is pushed per proven property
(assert (=> (> vars_length 0) (= checks_pushed 1)))

; Negation: a zero-var contract still produces a check
(assert (= vars_length 0))
(assert (> checks_pushed 0))

(check-sat)

results.description: State mutation between printHelp:537 and printHelp:538: VERSION = "0.3.0" is a compile-time constant (5 chars).
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 537
; VERSION = "0.3.0" is a compile-time constant (5 chars).
; The interpolated header "neurallog v0.3.0 — a logger that fixes your code"
; must therefore be provably non-empty.
(declare-const version_len Int)
(declare-const prefix_len Int)
(declare-const suffix_len Int)
(declare-const header_len Int)
; Code transition: VERSION literal "0.3.0" has exactly 5 characters
(assert (= version_len 5))
; Code transition: prefix "neurallog v" has exactly 11 characters
(assert (= prefix_len 11))
; Code transition: suffix " — a logger that fixes your code" is positive in length
(assert (> suffix_len 0))
; Code transition: header_len = prefix + VERSION + suffix
(assert (= header_len (+ prefix_len version_len suffix_len)))
; Negation of property: header is empty
(assert (<= header_len 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: State mutation between printHelp:537 and printHelp:538: Ordering constraint: printHelp contains zero if-statements a
results.smt2:

(declare-const return_stmt_count Int)
(declare-const skippable_calls Int)
; Code transition: AST of printHelp body has 0 if-statements

; Code transition: AST of printHelp body has 0 return-statements

(check-sat)

results.description: State mutation between runInit:97 and runInit:98: PROVEN: totalSignals >= fileCount at the time of the log.
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 97
; PROVEN: totalSignals >= fileCount at the time of the log.
; fileCount increments only when signals.length > 0, adding at least 1 to totalSignals.
(declare-const total_signals Int)
(declare-const file_count Int)
(declare-const n Int)  ; number of counted files
(declare-const s Int)  ; minimum signals per counted file
; Code transition: s >= 1 (guard: `if (signals.length > 0)`)
(assert (>= s 1))
; Code transition: file_count = n; total_signals >= n * s (each counted file contributes >= 1)
(assert (>= n 0))
(assert (= file_count n))
(assert (>= total_signals (* n s)))
; Negated property: totalSignals < fileCount

results.description: State mutation between extractFunctionName:140 and extractFunctionName:154: Proven: when all structural guards hold for the member_expre
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 140
; Proven: when all structural guards hold for the member_expression callback branch,
; the function returns a qualified (non-anonymous) name.

(declare-const parent_is_arguments       Int)  ; 1 = node.parent.type === "arguments"
(declare-const grandparent_is_call_expr  Int)  ; 1 = node.parent.parent.type === "call_expression"
(declare-const callfn_is_member_expr     Int)  ; 1 = callFn.type === "member_expression"
(declare-const method_nonnull            Int)  ; 1 = childForFieldName("property") != null
(declare-const object_not_call_expr      Int)  ; 1 = object.type !== "call_expression"
(declare-const object_text_nonnull       Int)  ; 1 = object.text is defined
(declare-const result_is_anonymous       Int)  ; 1 = result === "<anonymous>"

; Code transition: all structural guards pass (lines 141–143)
(assert (= parent_is_arguments      1))
(assert (= grandparent_is_call_expr 1))
(assert (= callfn_is_member_expr    1))
(assert (= method_nonnull           1))
(assert (= object_not_call_expr     1))

; Code transition: tree-sitter guarantees .text is defined for any non-null SyntaxNode
(assert (= object_text_nonnull 1))

; Code transition: line 150 executes return `${objName}.${method.text}`
; — a non-empty concatenation that can never equal "<anonymous>"
(assert (=> (and (= method_nonnull 1) (= object_text_nonnull 1))
            (= result_is_anonymous 0)))

; Negation of property: result IS anonymous — prove this is impossible
(assert (= result_is_anonymous 1))
(check-sat)

results.description: State mutation between extractFunctionName:154 and extractFunctionName:160: Proven: when callFn is a non-null identifier, the return val
results.smt2:

; The optional-chaining guard (callFn?.type === "identifier") ensures callFn is non-null
; within the branch body, and tree-sitter guarantees .text for any non-null node.

(declare-const callfn_nonnull             Int)  ; 1 = callFn != null
(declare-const callfn_type_is_identifier  Int)  ; 1 = callFn.type === "identifier"
(declare-const callfn_text_defined        Int)  ; 1 = callFn.text is defined
(declare-const result_has_callback_suffix Int)  ; 1 = result === "${callFn.text}.callback"

; Code transition: optional-chaining short-circuits on null, so within the branch:
(assert (= callfn_nonnull            1))
(assert (= callfn_type_is_identifier 1))

; Code transition: tree-sitter invariant — .text always defined for non-null SyntaxNode
(assert (= callfn_text_defined callfn_nonnull))

; Code transition: line 155 executes return `${callFn.text}.callback`
(assert (=> (and (= callfn_nonnull 1) (= callfn_text_defined 1))
            (= result_has_callback_suffix 1)))

; Negation: result does NOT have the ".callback" suffix
(assert (= result_has_callback_suffix 0))
(check-sat)

results.description: State mutation between extractPathConditions:237 and extractPathConditions:246: PROVEN: condition.text not added when logNode is in conditio

(declare-const log_in_condition Int)             ; 1 = logNode is inside condition subtree
(declare-const isDescendant_consequence Int)     ; result of isDescendantOf(logNode,consequence)
(declare-const condition_text_added Int)         ; 1 = condition.text pushed into conditions

(assert (= current_is_condition_child 1))
(assert (=> (= current_is_condition_child 1) (= log_in_condition 1)))

; Structural invariant: condition and consequence are disjoint subtrees
; logNode in condition => logNode NOT in consequence
(assert (=> (= log_in_condition 1) (= isDescendant_consequence 0)))

; Code transition: condition.text only added when isDescendantOf returns true
(assert (=> (= isDescendant_consequence 0) (= condition_text_added 0)))

(assert (= current_is_condition_child 1))
(assert (= condition_text_added 1))
(check-sat)
